forecaster_note: Hugh McDowell (Surfline): AM could go offshore again with a bit more residual S swell than forecast — winds light-moderate S, cold morning drainage. Head-high sets possible at S-facing spots, easing to waist-high in the sheltered corners through the day. UPDATE — unseasonal swell inbound: Sun sees an E swell build plus another pulse of solid S energy (needs wind shelter); Mon looks the pick of the week, head-to-overhead and clean.

wind.summary: Light SSE 10kph (gusts 17), technically cross-shore — cold morning air could tip it offshore for an hour or two at dawn.

shark.summary: No shark sightings in the last 24h at Dee Why, Long Reef, Curl Curl or Manly. SLSNSW drones grounded today at several nearby beaches (North Palm Beach, Whale Beach, Bilgola, Warriewood, North Curl Curl) — a routine scheduling notice, not shark-related.

days.label: Today
days.note: Small and average (poor-fair) across the board today — a brief AM offshore/light-wind window is possible per Surfline's forecaster before the SSE fills back in. Fine for a low-key paddle, not a priority session.

dee-why.window: AM could still go offshore per Surfline's forecaster — worth checking by 8–9am before the SSE fills back in. High tide 11:13am keeps the bank full through the morning, but it's mostly a small ESE windswell mix — nothing to prioritise.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: Small ESE/ENE mix lets the Point's rights trickle through — sit a little wider than usual and be patient for the occasional standout set.

long-reef.window: Same small ESE windswell as Dee Why, slightly better exposure off the reef. Fine for a wave count, nothing more — the swell isn't S enough yet to really feed this spot.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Not much on offer today — low-key backhand session if you just want reps.

curl-curl.window: Same story as Long Reef — small and average. The S-swell magnet isn't getting fed today since the energy is mostly ESE, not S.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Nothing special — a play session at best until real S swell shows up.

manly.window: Cross-shore keeps North Steyne a touch cleaner than the exposed beaches — worth a look if Dee Why blows out early.

tip.label: Backup option
tip.text: Sheltered corner takes the edge off the SSE wind — small but rideable if the exposed spots are too choppy.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: New ENE groundswell (7.3ft @ 11s from 74° — dead in your 30–80° sweet spot) pushes size to head-high-plus. SE wind stays onshore most of the day though, so Long Reef and Curl Curl get bumpy (poor-fair) despite the extra size. Dee Why Point and Manly hold shape better (fair) — worth an early check before the wind fills in.

dee-why.window: 74° ENE groundswell lands right in your best window — 7ft+ at 11s. SE wind stays onshore most of the day so it won't be glassy, but the Point handles size and angle well. High tide 11:59am keeps it full through late morning.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: This is the swell to prioritise this week — dead-on ENE angle for the Point's rights. Get in early or hunt the sheltered corners once the SE wind builds.

alerts: ⚠️ Onshore SE wind all session — needs shelter to be worth the extra size (per Surfline forecaster).
long-reef.window: Overhead-plus (2–5ft) but SE onshore chews it up here — poor-fair despite the size. Power without much shape.

alerts: ⚠️ Onshore SE wind all session — needs shelter to be worth the extra size (per Surfline forecaster).
curl-curl.window: Same as Long Reef — plenty of size but the SE onshore keeps it messy. Only for those chasing raw power over quality.

manly.window: Best wind shelter of the four tomorrow — cross-off keeps North Steyne rideable through the SE onshore. Good backup if the Point gets busy given the size.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Cross-off wind and the harbour-side corner shelter this from the worst of the SE chop — solid alternative if Dee Why's crowded.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Looking ahead to next weekend (25–26 Jul), not tomorrow — since today is already Saturday, 'tomorrow' already covers this Sunday. A dead-south swell (3.7ft @ 11s @ 188°) dominates over a small ENE filler. Long Reef and Curl Curl, both tuned for S swell, pick up the best ratings (fair-good/fair). Dee Why and Manly face more square into the dead-south angle and lose energy on the wrap, staying just fair. Light W offshore holds it together across the board.

alerts: ⚠️ Dominant swell is dead-south (188°) — wraps poorly at Dee Why, most of that energy is lost off the angle.
dee-why.window: Dead-south swell dominates over a weak ENE filler — most of that 3.7ft energy wraps away from the Point rather than into it. Light W offshore keeps it clean but there's not a lot to work with.

long-reef.window: Best pick of the four next weekend — the reef takes S swell well, and this 3.7ft/11s line lines up nicely. W offshore keeps it clean through the session.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: S-facing reef eating the dead-south line everyone else is wasting — prioritise this one over Dee Why.

curl-curl.window: The S-swell magnet earns its name here — decent size and shape off the dead-south line that Dee Why and Manly waste. Offshore and clean.

manly.window: Smallest of the four — the corner blocks most of the S energy, leaving just the tiny ENE filler. Clean but low-key.
